1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم الر ۚ تلك آيات الكتاب وقرآن مبين
1.english: Alif, Lam, Ra. These are the verses of the Book and a clear Qur'an.
1.transliteration: Alif-Laaam-Raa; tilka Aayaatul Kitaabi wa Qur-aa-nim Mubeen

1.arabic: ربما يود الذين كفروا لو كانوا مسلمين
1.english: Perhaps those who disbelieve will wish that they had been Muslims.
1.transliteration: Rubamaa yawaddul lazeena kafaroo law kaanoo muslimeen

1.arabic: ذرهم يأكلوا ويتمتعوا ويلههم الأمل ۖ فسوف يعلمون
1.english: Let them eat and enjoy themselves and be diverted by [false] hope, for they are going to know.
1.transliteration: Zarhum yaakuloo wa tatamatta'oo wa yulhihimul amalu fasawfa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: وما أهلكنا من قرية إلا ولها كتاب معلوم
1.english: And We did not destroy any city but that for it was a known decree.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa ahlaknaa min qaryatin illaa wa lahaa kitaabum ma'loom

1.arabic: ما تسبق من أمة أجلها وما يستأخرون
1.english: No nation will precede its term, nor will they remain thereafter.
1.transliteration: Maa tasbiqu min ummatin ajalahaa wa maa yastaakhiroon

1.arabic: وقالوا يا أيها الذي نزل عليه الذكر إنك لمجنون
1.english: And they say, "O you upon whom the message has been sent down, indeed you are mad.
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo yaaa aiyuhal lazee nuzzila 'alaihiz Zikru innaka lamajnoon

1.arabic: لو ما تأتينا بالملائكة إن كنت من الصادقين
1.english: Why do you not bring us the angels, if you should be among the truthful?"
1.transliteration: Law maa taateenaa bil malaaa'ikati in kunta minas saadiqeen

1.arabic: ما ننزل الملائكة إلا بالحق وما كانوا إذا منظرين
1.english: We do not send down the angels except with truth; and the disbelievers would not then be reprieved.
1.transliteration: Maa nunazzilul malaaa'i kata illaa bilhaqqi wa maa kaanooo izam munzareen

1.arabic: إنا نحن نزلنا الذكر وإنا له لحافظون
1.english: Indeed, it is We who sent down the Qur'an and indeed, We will be its guardian.
1.transliteration: Innaa Nahnu nazalnaz Zikra wa Innaa lahoo lahaa fizoon

1.arabic: ولقد أرسلنا من قبلك في شيع الأولين
1.english: And We had certainly sent [messengers] before you, [O Muhammad], among the sects of the former peoples.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad arsalnaa min qablika fee shiya'il awwaleen

1.arabic: وما يأتيهم من رسول إلا كانوا به يستهزئون
1.english: And no messenger would come to them except that they ridiculed him.
1.transliteration: Wa maa yaateehim mir Rasoolin illaa kaanoo bihee yastahzi'oon

1.arabic: كذلك نسلكه في قلوب المجرمين
1.english: Thus do We insert denial into the hearts of the criminals.
1.transliteration: kazaalika naslukuhoo fee quloobil mujrimeen

1.arabic: لا يؤمنون به ۖ وقد خلت سنة الأولين
1.english: They will not believe in it, while there has already occurred the precedent of the former peoples.
1.transliteration: Laa yu'minoona bihee wa qad khalat sunnatul awwaleen

1.arabic: ولو فتحنا عليهم بابا من السماء فظلوا فيه يعرجون
1.english: And [even] if We opened to them a gate from the heaven and they continued therein to ascend,
1.transliteration: Wa law fatahnaa 'alaihim baabam minas samaaa'i fazaloo feehi ya'rujoon

1.arabic: لقالوا إنما سكرت أبصارنا بل نحن قوم مسحورون
1.english: They would say, "Our eyes have only been dazzled. Rather, we are a people affected by magic."
1.transliteration: Laqaaloo innamaa sukkirat absaarunaa bal nahnu qawmum mashooroon

1.arabic: ولقد جعلنا في السماء بروجا وزيناها للناظرين
1.english: And We have placed within the heaven great stars and have beautified it for the observers.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad ja'alnaa fissamaaa'i buroojanw wa zaiyannaahaa linnaazireen

1.arabic: وحفظناها من كل شيطان رجيم
1.english: And We have protected it from every devil expelled [from the mercy of Allah]
1.transliteration: Wa hafiznaahaa min kulli Shaitaanir rajeem

1.arabic: إلا من استرق السمع فأتبعه شهاب مبين
1.english: Except one who steals a hearing and is pursued by a clear burning flame.
1.transliteration: Illaa manis taraqas sam'a fa atba'ahoo shihaabum mubeen

1.arabic: والأرض مددناها وألقينا فيها رواسي وأنبتنا فيها من كل شيء موزون
1.english: And the earth - We have spread it and cast therein firmly set mountains and caused to grow therein [something] of every well-balanced thing.
1.transliteration: Wal arda madadnaahaa wa alqainaa feehaa rawaasiya wa ambatnaa feehaa min kulli shai'im mawzoon

1.arabic: وجعلنا لكم فيها معايش ومن لستم له برازقين
1.english: And We have made for you therein means of living and [for] those for whom you are not providers.
1.transliteration: Wa ja'alnaa lakum feehaa ma'aayisha wa mal lastum lahoo biraaziqeen

1.arabic: وإن من شيء إلا عندنا خزائنه وما ننزله إلا بقدر معلوم
1.english: And there is not a thing but that with Us are its depositories, and We do not send it down except according to a known measure.
1.transliteration: Wa im min shai'in illaa 'indanaa khazaaa 'inuhoo wa maa nunazziluhooo illaa biqadarim ma'loom

1.arabic: وأرسلنا الرياح لواقح فأنزلنا من السماء ماء فأسقيناكموه وما أنتم له بخازنين
1.english: And We have sent the fertilizing winds and sent down water from the sky and given you drink from it. And you are not its retainers.
1.transliteration: Wa arsalnar riyaaha la waaqiha fa anzalnaa minas samaaa'i maaa'an fa asqai naakumoohu wa maaa antum lahoo bikhaazineen

1.arabic: وإنا لنحن نحيي ونميت ونحن الوارثون
1.english: And indeed, it is We who give life and cause death, and We are the Inheritor.
1.transliteration: Wa innnaa la nahnu nuhyee wa numeetu wa nahnul waarisoon

1.arabic: ولقد علمنا المستقدمين منكم ولقد علمنا المستأخرين
1.english: And We have already known the preceding [generations] among you, and We have already known the later [ones to come].
1.transliteration: Wa la qad 'alimnal mustaqdimeena minkum wa laqad 'alimnal mustaakhireen

1.arabic: وإن ربك هو يحشرهم ۚ إنه حكيم عليم
1.english: And indeed, your Lord will gather them; indeed, He is Wise and Knowing.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka Huwa yahshuruhum; innahoo Hakeem 'Aleem

1.arabic: ولقد خلقنا الإنسان من صلصال من حمإ مسنون
1.english: And We did certainly create man out of clay from an altered black mud.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad khalaqnal insaana min salsaalim min hama im masnoon

1.arabic: والجان خلقناه من قبل من نار السموم
1.english: And the jinn We created before from scorching fire.
1.transliteration: Waljaaanna khalaqnaahu min qablu min naaris samoom

1.arabic: وإذ قال ربك للملائكة إني خالق بشرا من صلصال من حمإ مسنون
1.english: And [mention, O Muhammad], when your Lord said to the angels, "I will create a human being out of clay from an altered black mud.
1.transliteration: Wa iz qaala Rabbuka lilmalaaa' ikati innee khaaliqum basharam min salsaalim min hama im masnoon

1.arabic: فإذا سويته ونفخت فيه من روحي فقعوا له ساجدين
1.english: And when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My [created] soul, then fall down to him in prostration."
1.transliteration: Fa izaa sawwaituhoo wa nafakhtu feehi mir roohee faqa'oo lahoo saajideen

1.arabic: فسجد الملائكة كلهم أجمعون
1.english: So the angels prostrated - all of them entirely,
1.transliteration: Fasajadal malaaa'ikatu kulluhum ajma'oon

1.arabic: إلا إبليس أبى أن يكون مع الساجدين
1.english: Except Iblees, he refused to be with those who prostrated.
1.transliteration: Illaaa ibleesa abaaa ai yakoona ma'as saajideen

1.arabic: قال يا إبليس ما لك ألا تكون مع الساجدين
1.english: [Allah] said, O Iblees, what is [the matter] with you that you are not with those who prostrate?"
1.transliteration: Qaala yaaa Ibleesu maa laka allaa takoona ma'as saajideen

1.arabic: قال لم أكن لأسجد لبشر خلقته من صلصال من حمإ مسنون
1.english: He said, "Never would I prostrate to a human whom You created out of clay from an altered black mud."
1.transliteration: Qaala lam akul li asjuda libasharin khalaqtahoo min salsaalim min hama im masnoon

1.arabic: قال فاخرج منها فإنك رجيم
1.english: [Allah] said, "Then get out of it, for indeed, you are expelled.
1.transliteration: Qaala fakhruj minhaa fa innaka rajeem

1.arabic: وإن عليك اللعنة إلى يوم الدين
1.english: And indeed, upon you is the curse until the Day of Recompense."
1.transliteration: Wa inna 'alikal la'nata ilaa Yawmid Deen

1.arabic: قال رب فأنظرني إلى يوم يبعثون
1.english: He said, "My Lord, then reprieve me until the Day they are resurrected."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbi fa anzirneee ilaa Yawmi yub'asoon

1.arabic: قال فإنك من المنظرين
1.english: [Allah] said, "So indeed, you are of those reprieved
1.transliteration: Qaala fa innaka minal munzareen

1.arabic: إلى يوم الوقت المعلوم
1.english: Until the Day of the time well-known."
1.transliteration: Ilaa Yawmil waqtil ma'loom

1.arabic: قال رب بما أغويتني لأزينن لهم في الأرض ولأغوينهم أجمعين
1.english: [Iblees] said, "My Lord, because You have put me in error, I will surely make [disobedience] attractive to them on earth, and I will mislead them all
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbi bimaaa aghwaitanee la uzayyinaana lahum fil ardi wa la ughwiyan nahum ajma'een

1.arabic: إلا عبادك منهم المخلصين
1.english: Except, among them, Your chosen servants."
1.transliteration: Illaa 'ibaadaka minhumul mukhlaseen

1.arabic: قال هذا صراط علي مستقيم
1.english: [Allah] said, "This is a path [of return] to Me [that is] straight.
1.transliteration: Qaala haaza Siraatun 'alaiya Mustaqeem

1.arabic: إن عبادي ليس لك عليهم سلطان إلا من اتبعك من الغاوين
1.english: Indeed, My servants - no authority will you have over them, except those who follow you of the deviators.
1.transliteration: Inna 'ibaadee laisa laka 'alaihim sultaanun illaa manittaba'aka minal ghaaween

1.arabic: وإن جهنم لموعدهم أجمعين
1.english: And indeed, Hell is the promised place for them all.
1.transliteration: Wa inna jahannama lamaw'iduhum ajma'een

1.arabic: لها سبعة أبواب لكل باب منهم جزء مقسوم
1.english: It has seven gates; for every gate is of them a portion designated."
1.transliteration: Lahaa sab'atu abwaab; likulli baabim minhum juz'um maqsoom

1.arabic: إن المتقين في جنات وعيون
1.english: Indeed, the righteous will be within gardens and springs.
1.transliteration: Innal muttaqeena fee Jannaatinw wa 'uyoon

1.arabic: ادخلوها بسلام آمنين
1.english: [Having been told], "Enter it in peace, safe [and secure]."
1.transliteration: Udkhuloohaa bisalaamin aamineen

1.arabic: ونزعنا ما في صدورهم من غل إخوانا على سرر متقابلين
1.english: And We will remove whatever is in their breasts of resentment, [so they will be] brothers, on thrones facing each other.
1.transliteration: Wa naza'naa ma fee sudoorihim min ghillin ikhwaanan 'alaa sururim mutaqaabileen

1.arabic: لا يمسهم فيها نصب وما هم منها بمخرجين
1.english: No fatigue will touch them therein, nor from it will they [ever] be removed.
1.transliteration: Laa yamas suhum feehaa nasabunw wa maa hum minhaa bimukhrajeen

1.arabic: ۞ نبئ عبادي أني أنا الغفور الرحيم
1.english: [O Muhammad], inform My servants that it is I who am the Forgiving, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Nabbi' 'ibaadeee annneee anal Ghafoorur Raheem

1.arabic: وأن عذابي هو العذاب الأليم
1.english: And that it is My punishment which is the painful punishment.
1.transliteration: Wa anna 'azaabee uwal 'azaabul aleem

1.arabic: ونبئهم عن ضيف إبراهيم
1.english: And inform them about the guests of Abraham,
1.transliteration: Wa nabbi'hum 'an daifi Ibraaheem

1.arabic: إذ دخلوا عليه فقالوا سلاما قال إنا منكم وجلون
1.english: When they entered upon him and said, "Peace." [Abraham] said, "Indeed, we are fearful of you."
1.transliteration: Iz dakhaloo 'alaihi faqaaloo salaaman qaala innaa minkum wajiloon

1.arabic: قالوا لا توجل إنا نبشرك بغلام عليم
1.english: [The angels] said, "Fear not. Indeed, we give you good tidings of a learned boy."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo la tawjal innaa nubashshiruka bighulaamin 'aleem

1.arabic: قال أبشرتموني على أن مسني الكبر فبم تبشرون
1.english: He said, "Have you given me good tidings although old age has come upon me? Then of what [wonder] do you inform?"
1.transliteration: Qaala abashshartumoonee 'alaaa am massaniyal kibaru fabima tubashshiroon

1.arabic: قالوا بشرناك بالحق فلا تكن من القانطين
1.english: They said, "We have given you good tidings in truth, so do not be of the despairing."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo bashsharnaaka bilhaqqi falaa takum minal qaaniteen

1.arabic: قال ومن يقنط من رحمة ربه إلا الضالون
1.english: He said, "And who despairs of the mercy of his Lord except for those astray?"
1.transliteration: Qaala wa mai yaqnatu mir rahmati Rabbiheee illad daaaloon

1.arabic: قال فما خطبكم أيها المرسلون
1.english: [Abraham] said, "Then what is your business [here], O messengers?"
1.transliteration: Qaala famaa khatbukum aiyuhal mursaloon

1.arabic: قالوا إنا أرسلنا إلى قوم مجرمين
1.english: They said, "Indeed, we have been sent to a people of criminals,
1.transliteration: Qaaloo innaaa ursilnaaa ilaa qawmim mujrimeen

1.arabic: إلا آل لوط إنا لمنجوهم أجمعين
1.english: Except the family of Lot; indeed, we will save them all
1.transliteration: Illaaa Aala Loot; innaa lamunajjoohum ajma'een

1.arabic: إلا امرأته قدرنا ۙ إنها لمن الغابرين
1.english: Except his wife." Allah decreed that she is of those who remain behind.
1.transliteration: Illam ra atahoo qaddarnaaa innahaa laminal ghaabireen

1.arabic: فلما جاء آل لوط المرسلون
1.english: And when the messengers came to the family of Lot,
1.transliteration: Falamma jaaa'a Aala Lootinil mursaloon

1.arabic: قال إنكم قوم منكرون
1.english: He said, "Indeed, you are people unknown."
1.transliteration: Qaala innakum qawmum munkaroon

1.arabic: قالوا بل جئناك بما كانوا فيه يمترون
1.english: They said, "But we have come to you with that about which they were disputing,
1.transliteration: Qaaloo bal ji'naaka bimaa kaanoo feehi yamtaroon

1.arabic: وأتيناك بالحق وإنا لصادقون
1.english: And we have come to you with truth, and indeed, we are truthful.
1.transliteration: Wa atainaaka bilhaqqi wa innaa lasaadiqoon

1.arabic: فأسر بأهلك بقطع من الليل واتبع أدبارهم ولا يلتفت منكم أحد وامضوا حيث تؤمرون
1.english: So set out with your family during a portion of the night and follow behind them and let not anyone among you look back and continue on to where you are commanded."
1.transliteration: Fa asri bi ahlika biqit'im minal laili wattabi' adbaarahum wa laa yaltafit minkum ahadunw wamdoo haisu tu'maroon

1.arabic: وقضينا إليه ذلك الأمر أن دابر هؤلاء مقطوع مصبحين
1.english: And We conveyed to him [the decree] of that matter: that those [sinners] would be eliminated by early morning.
1.transliteration: Wa qadainaaa ilaihi zaalikal amra anna daabira haaa'ulaaa'i maqtoo'um musbiheen

1.arabic: وجاء أهل المدينة يستبشرون
1.english: And the people of the city came rejoicing.
1.transliteration: Wa jaaa'a ahlul madeenati yastabshiroon

1.arabic: قال إن هؤلاء ضيفي فلا تفضحون
1.english: [Lot] said, "Indeed, these are my guests, so do not shame me.
1.transliteration: Qaala inna haaa'ulaaa'i daifee falaa tafdahoon

1.arabic: واتقوا الله ولا تخزون
1.english: And fear Allah and do not disgrace me."
1.transliteration: Wattaqul laaha wa laa tukhzoon

1.arabic: قالوا أولم ننهك عن العالمين
1.english: They said, "Have we not forbidden you from [protecting] people?"
1.transliteration: Qaalooo awalam nanhaka 'anil 'aalameen

1.arabic: قال هؤلاء بناتي إن كنتم فاعلين
1.english: [Lot] said, "These are my daughters - if you would be doers [of lawful marriage]."
1.transliteration: Qaala haaa'ulaaa'i banaateee in kuntum faa'ileen

1.arabic: لعمرك إنهم لفي سكرتهم يعمهون
1.english: By your life, [O Muhammad], indeed they were, in their intoxication, wandering blindly.
1.transliteration: La'amruka innahum lafee sakratihim ya'mahoon

1.arabic: فأخذتهم الصيحة مشرقين
1.english: So the shriek seized them at sunrise.
1.transliteration: Fa akhazat humus saihatu mushriqeen

1.arabic: فجعلنا عاليها سافلها وأمطرنا عليهم حجارة من سجيل
1.english: And We made the highest part [of the city] its lowest and rained upon them stones of hard clay.
1.transliteration: Faja'alnaa 'aaliyahaa saafilahaa wa amtamaa 'alaihim hijaaratam min sjijjeel

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لآيات للمتوسمين
1.english: Indeed in that are signs for those who discern.
1.transliteration: Inna fee zaalika la Aayaatil lilmutawassimeen

1.arabic: وإنها لبسبيل مقيم
1.english: And indeed, those cities are [situated] on an established road.
1.transliteration: Wa innahaa labi sabeelim muqeem

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لآية للمؤمنين
1.english: Indeed in that is a sign for the believers.
1.transliteration: Inna fee zaalika la Aayatal lilmu'mineen

1.arabic: وإن كان أصحاب الأيكة لظالمين
1.english: And the companions of the thicket were [also] wrongdoers.
1.transliteration: Wa in kaana Ashaabul Aikati lazaalimeen

1.arabic: فانتقمنا منهم وإنهما لبإمام مبين
1.english: So We took retribution from them, and indeed, both [cities] are on a clear highway.
1.transliteration: Fantaqamnaa minhum wa innahumaa labi imaamim mubeen

1.arabic: ولقد كذب أصحاب الحجر المرسلين
1.english: And certainly did the companions of Thamud deny the messengers.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad kazzaba ashaabul Hijril mursaleen

1.arabic: وآتيناهم آياتنا فكانوا عنها معرضين
1.english: And We gave them Our signs, but from them they were turning away.
1.transliteration: Wa aatainaahum Aayaatinaa fakaanoo 'anhaa mu'rideen

1.arabic: وكانوا ينحتون من الجبال بيوتا آمنين
1.english: And they used to carve from the mountains, houses, feeling secure.
1.transliteration: Wa kaanoo yanhitoona minal jibaali buyootan aamineen

1.arabic: فأخذتهم الصيحة مصبحين
1.english: But the shriek seized them at early morning.
1.transliteration: Fa akhazat humus saihatu musbiheen

1.arabic: فما أغنى عنهم ما كانوا يكسبون
1.english: So nothing availed them [from] what they used to earn.
1.transliteration: Famaaa aghnaa 'anhum maa kaanoo yaksiboon

1.arabic: وما خلقنا السماوات والأرض وما بينهما إلا بالحق ۗ وإن الساعة لآتية ۖ فاصفح الصفح الجميل
1.english: And We have not created the heavens and earth and that between them except in truth. And indeed, the Hour is coming; so forgive with gracious forgiveness.
1.transliteration: Wa maa khalaqnas samaawaati wal arda wa maa bainahumaaa illaa bilhaqq; wa innas Saa'ata la aatiyatun fasfahis safhal jameel

1.arabic: إن ربك هو الخلاق العليم
1.english: Indeed, your Lord - He is the Knowing Creator.
1.transliteration: Inna Rabbaka Huwal khallaaqul 'aleem

1.arabic: ولقد آتيناك سبعا من المثاني والقرآن العظيم
1.english: And We have certainly given you, [O Muhammad], seven of the often repeated [verses] and the great Qur'an.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aatainaaka sab'am mnal masaanee wal Qur-aanal 'azeem

1.arabic: لا تمدن عينيك إلى ما متعنا به أزواجا منهم ولا تحزن عليهم واخفض جناحك للمؤمنين
1.english: Do not extend your eyes toward that by which We have given enjoyment to [certain] categories of the disbelievers, and do not grieve over them. And lower your wing to the believers
1.transliteration: Laa tamuddanna 'ainaika ilaa maa matta 'naa biheee azwaajam minhum wa laa tahzan 'alaihim wakhfid janaahaka lilmu 'mineen

1.arabic: وقل إني أنا النذير المبين
1.english: And say, "Indeed, I am the clear warner" -
1.transliteration: Wa qul inneee anan nazeerul mubeen

1.arabic: كما أنزلنا على المقتسمين
1.english: Just as We had revealed [scriptures] to the separators
1.transliteration: Kamaaa anzalnaa 'alal muqtasimeen

1.arabic: الذين جعلوا القرآن عضين
1.english: Who have made the Qur'an into portions.
1.transliteration: Allazeena ja'alul Quraana'ideen

1.arabic: فوربك لنسألنهم أجمعين
1.english: So by your Lord, We will surely question them all
1.transliteration: Fawa Rabbika lanas'a lannahum ajma'een

1.arabic: عما كانوا يعملون
1.english: About what they used to do.
1.transliteration: 'Ammaa kaanoo ya'maloon

1.arabic: فاصدع بما تؤمر وأعرض عن المشركين
1.english: Then declare what you are commanded and turn away from the polytheists.
1.transliteration: Fasda' bimaa tu'maru wa a'rid anil mushrikeen

1.arabic: إنا كفيناك المستهزئين
1.english: Indeed, We are sufficient for you against the mockers
1.transliteration: Innaa kafainaakal mustahzi'een

1.arabic: الذين يجعلون مع الله إلها آخر ۚ فسوف يعلمون
1.english: Who make [equal] with Allah another deity. But they are going to know.
1.transliteration: Allazeena yaj'aloona ma'al laahi ilaahan aakhar; fasawfa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: ولقد نعلم أنك يضيق صدرك بما يقولون
1.english: And We already know that your breast is constrained by what they say.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad na'lamu annak yadeequ sadruka bimaa yaqooloon

1.arabic: فسبح بحمد ربك وكن من الساجدين
1.english: So exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord and be of those who prostrate [to Him].
1.transliteration: Fasbbih bihamdi Rabbika wa kum minas saajideen

1.arabic: واعبد ربك حتى يأتيك اليقين
1.english: And worship your Lord until there comes to you the certainty (death).
1.transliteration: Wa'bud Rabbaka hattaa yaatiyakal yaqeen
